title: Filed arc epilogue and replay intent
summary: After High Pass is filed, give the player a calm reason to revisit without undoing the completed-arc feeling.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: The filed arc has a settled epilogue state and optional replay intent that never reads like unfinished required work.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Add a compact completed-arc resolver or station subtitle state that separates done, revisit, and future-scope-gated language.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Write one filed-arc synthesis/epilogue line that celebrates noticing relationships without promising season three.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Tune the final return frame or station-adjacent calm accent only if it improves the endcap proof.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Make replay hooks explicit and optional: replay should invite noticing changed conditions, not reopen filed tasks.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.focus: Make the filed-arc replay contract explicit as a regression guard: optional revisit copy stays on the filed expedition surface, while route replay hooks remain inactive after High Pass is filed.
current_state: filed High Pass already suppresses active field request, active outing, route marker, world-map replay label, journal field request, and route-board replay notes
lane 1 added explicit filed-arc copy buckets, including optional revisit copy
lane 2 refreshed the station homecoming epilogue line without changing replay state
main_acceptance: filed High Pass route-board tests explicitly assert `complete: true`, `targetBiomeId: null`, `notebookReady: null`, and `replayNote: null`

field-request state tests explicitly assert no active request, active outing, journal request, route marker location, or world-map route replay label after filed High Pass, even with route-marker selected and Treeline focused
snapshot or runtime coverage confirms the filed expedition notice remains the optional revisit invitation and does not create a task
a paired pre-filed / active High Pass expectation still proves `Today: High Pass` can appear before filing

no player-facing copy drift, save schema, route definition, station page/UI, route filing behavior, support behavior, geometry, season-three promise, new route task, new replay UI, or authored science/copy change
baseline_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "replay|filed High Pass|High Pass filed"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "season outing locator|High Pass locator|High Pass filed"
main_verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "files High Pass|High Pass filed|filed High Pass" only if runtime smoke expectations are touched

main_result.summary: Added test-only regression coverage for filed High Pass replay intent: the route board now explicitly has no target, no notebook-ready return, and no replay note; field-request state has no active task, outing, journal request, hint, marker, or replay label; filed snapshots keep the optional revisit invitation on the expedition surface.
guardrails_preserved: no player-facing copy, save schema, route definition, station page/UI, route filing behavior, support behavior, geometry, season-three promise, new route task, new replay UI, or authored science/copy change
pre-filed and active High Pass can still show `Today: High Pass`, while filed High Pass cannot
optional revisit intent remains on the filed expedition surface

critic_result.summary: Confirmed the lane-4 implementation stayed test-only and locks filed High Pass away from active route, journal, marker, hint, notebook-ready, replay-note, and world-map replay surfaces while keeping optional revisit copy on the filed expedition surface.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

lane_1_scout_refinement.focus: Extract the existing filed High Pass state copy into a compact typed resolver so done, optional revisit, and future-scope-gated language stay explicit without copy drift.

copy_buckets.filed_location: High Pass filed from Treeline Pass.
copy_buckets.field_arc_complete: High Pass filed. This field arc is complete.
copy_buckets.optional_revisit: Current field arc filed. Revisit when you want a quiet pass.
copy_buckets.filed_expedition_subtitle: High Pass is filed for this field arc.
copy_buckets.notice_text: High Pass filed from Treeline Pass. Current field arc filed. Revisit when you want a quiet pass.

acceptance: The filed High Pass resolver separates filed-location, field-arc-complete, optional-revisit, and filed-expedition-subtitle copy without player-facing copy drift.
Filed High Pass still suppresses `resolveSeasonOutingLocator`, active field request, route marker, route replay label, and journal field request.
Routes page, archive strip, atlas note, launch card, expedition card, and expedition notice still render the same exact filed lines.
The lane-2 homecoming epilogue line remains `High Pass filed. Revisit how stone, shelter, and talus connect.`
No save schema, route definition, support behavior, station page/UI, geometry, season-three promise, new route task, or science-copy change lands in this pass.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "files High Pass|High Pass filed|filed High Pass"

lane_1_main_result.summary: Added a typed filed High Pass copy resolver and reused it in the filed chapter state plus High Pass archive subtitle special case, preserving exact visible filed lines while separating completion, optional revisit, and filed-expedition subtitle intent.
verified: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "files High Pass|High Pass filed|filed High Pass"
direct filed debug-snapshot browser state capture in output/web-game/filed-arc-epilogue-state-main-354-filed/
lane_1_main_result.validation_note: npm run validate:agents passes with the existing work-queue-size warning; packet 137 parses cleanly.
non_goals_preserved: no player-facing filed High Pass copy drift
no save schema or route definition change
no support behavior, station page/UI, geometry, season-three promise, new route task, or science-copy change

lane_1_critic_result.summary: Confirmed the filed High Pass copy resolver keeps completion, optional revisit, and filed-subtitle intent explicit without changing visible strings or reopening filed-route behavior.
checks: Filed High Pass still has no active outing, active field request, route marker, route replay label, or journal field request.
Routes page, archive strip, atlas note, launch card, expedition card, and notice text still render the same filed strings.

checks: The new line is exact-copy tested through the chronological homecoming milestone case.
The new line is 63 characters, under the existing 76-character homecoming text budget.
